title: Destination Canada Mobility Forum activities: Hire Francophone and bilingual talent - Canada.ca
content:
## Destination Canada Mobility Forum activities: Hire Francophone and bilingual talent
- Dates and locations of the activities
- Register for the event
- Other international recruitment opportunities
Canadian employers with positions to fill in provinces and territories other than Quebec can participate as exhibitors in information, networking and recruitment events.
Our events give you access to a pool of qualified candidates from around the world.
Some of the candidates invited to participate will have a work permit or permanent residence, but the majority will not. They will be eligible for a facilitated work permit under the francophone stream . This stream allows you to hire Francophone or bilingual foreign nationals for positions outside Quebec without having to get a labour market impact assessment.
Employers who can’t participate directly can share their job offers with exhibitors from
- their province or territory
- their city or region
- an economic development agency
- the Réseau de développement économique et d’employabilité (available in French only)
These exhibitors will be able to refer candidates who meet the requirements for your positions.

## Dates and locations of the activities

### 21st edition of the Destination Canada Mobility Forum
These activities will take place
- online on February 2, 3 and 4, 2026
- in Tunis, Tunisia, on February 9, 10 and 11, 2026
- in Paris, France, on February 14, 2026
For the in-person activities, we provide a booth free of charge.
For the online events, we offer access to a digital platform with a space for virtual booths and technical support before and during the forum.
Candidates will apply to job offers using the contact information indicated on the offers.
We will ask you to provide feedback on the candidates and inform us of any hires made during the Destination Canada Mobility Forum.

## Register for the event
Registration is now open .
In your email, make sure to include
- that you’re interested in participating in this event, and
- the name of your organization.
Employers wishing to participate as exhibitors agree to take part in all three segments:
Participation is not intended for recruitment agencies, lawyers, or immigration consultants.

## Other international recruitment opportunities
Contact the Dedicated Service Channel
You can complete a job profile form (PDF, 339 KB) and send it to the Dedicated Service Channel at any time.
We can connect you to public employment agencies (PDF, 1.2 MB) in French-speaking countries for job posting and candidate selection. The services of the public employment agencies are free of charge for employers and candidates.
Find out more information on hiring French-speaking or bilingual workers .

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
